info.name: Suprema CLUe API
info.description:
All requests use the `https://api.moon.supremainc.com/v1` and `{{apiKey}}` collection variables. Set `{{apiKey}}` to the API key issued from the CLUe Web Portal (Place Group → Settings → Admin → Manage API Keys); it is sent as a Bearer token via the collection-level Authorization setting, inherited by every request.

For more information on issuing CLUe API keys, see [CLUe API Guide](../platform/clue/clue-api-guide).

## API Basics

**Base URL:** `https://api.moon.supremainc.com/v1` — all requests must be made over HTTPS.

CLUe follows RESTful principles, using `GET`, `POST`, `PATCH`, and `DELETE`. Request and response bodies are JSON-encoded.

**Success response**: HTTP `200`

| field | Desc | Optional |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `code` | API Response Code | |
| `status` | API Response Status | |
| `message` | API Response Message | true |
| `data` | API Response Data | true |

**Fail response**: HTTP `400` / `500`

| field | Desc | Optional |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `errors` | API fail Response | |
| `errors.code` | Fail error code | |
| `errors.message` | Fail reference message | |
| `errors.details` | Fail details | true |


#### General Error Codes

| code | desc |
| --- | --- |
| `e1000` | Internal Server Error |
| `e1001` | Permission Denied |
| `e1002` | Not Supported |
| `e1003` | Invalid Format |
| `e1004` | Json Parse Error |
| `e1005` | Method Argument Not Valid |
| `e1006` | Bad Request |
| `e1007` | Resource Not Exists |
| `e1008` | Method Argument Type Mismatch |
| `e1009` | Missing Request Header |
| `e1010` | TooManyRequest |

## API Limit

CLUe has an API call traffic limit of **300 calls per 5 minutes** per API, per source. For face enrollment specifically, add some sleep time between calls to account for template extraction and device sync.

## Folders in this collection

- **User & Face Management** — create users, enroll faces, and pre-check duplicate faces via device.
- **User & Card Management** — enroll CSN/Wiegand cards, manage doors, and modify/delete auths.
- **Fingerprint Enrollment** — enroll a fingerprint via device scan.
- **RF Card Enrollment** — enroll an RF card via device scan.
- **Door Control** — remote unlock/lock.
- **Device Display** — device images and on-screen notifications.
- **Event Logs** — retrieve access/device/place event logs.
- **Dynamic QR Code** — generate/list/retrieve/delete CLUe dynamic QR codes for VENDOR-integrated access.

item.name: User & Face Management

request.description:
Creates a CLUe user and enrolls a face credential for the given place.

A face-supported Suprema device must already be added to the place, and a door must be configured for use through the CLUe web portal before calling this API.

- **user** refers to CLUe's internal management for global user administration.
- **auth** includes credentials such as face images and card IDs, which are specific to the given place.

#### Header request

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Authorization | JWT AccessToken (Bearer {{apiKey}}) |

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| placeId | placeId |

#### Body notes

- `user.key`: required, globally unique, 1-64 chars, lowercase letters/numbers and `- _ @ .` only. Use your company initial as a prefix.
- `auth.startTime` / `auth.endTime`: access allow period (epoch ms). Do not exceed Feb 2038 for endTime.
- `auth.doorAccessType`: `ALL_DOOR` or `SPECIFIC` (with `auth.doorIds`, e.g. `[1, 2]`, found via the CLUe web portal or the Door List API).
- `auth.scheduleType`: `ALWAYS` allows access at all times; `SPECIFIC` restricts access to a defined weekday schedule.
   - When `auth.scheduleType` is `SPECIFIC`, also set `auth.scheduleId` to the ID of a schedule created in advance via the CLUe web portal.
- `auth.credentials.name`: shown on the device display if entered.
- `auth.credentials.faceImages`: base64 encoded face image(s), up to two allowed.
- `auth.activeStatus`: mandatory, `ACTIVE` or inactive to disable.

**Name field REGEX:** `^(?=.{1,50}$)[\p{L}\p{N}\-ー_・\uFF66-\uFF9D]+(?:[ 　][\p{L}\p{N}\-ー_・\uFF66-\uFF9D]+)*$`

> When calling this API repeatedly (bulk enrollment), set at least 300ms between calls to allow time for template extraction and device sync.

#### Error Response codes (face quality)

| code | desc |
| --- | --- |
| f1000 | FaceHeadPoseAngleExceedThreshold (Tilted face) |
| f1001 | FaceImageQualityTooLow |
| f1002 | FaceDetectFailed |

#### Rate limit

Face enrollment/modification requests are rate-limited. When the limit is exceeded, the API returns an error together with a `Retry-After` header indicating how many seconds to wait before retrying.

| Situation | HTTP status | code | desc |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Caller exceeded its own request limit | 400 | `e1010` | TooManyRequest |
| Server-wide face template extraction is congested | 500 | `f1006` | FaceExtractionBusy |

> On receiving either error, wait for the number of seconds given in the `Retry-After` header, then retry the request.

query.description: The unique id of the place.

request.description:
Returns the list of devices in a place that support server-assisted duplicate face pre-check.

The server does not perform face matching itself (storing/maintaining face templates for every place in memory would be costly). Instead, face matching is executed on connected devices. This assumes all users to be checked are already synced to the target device and that the device is online.

> Requires BS3 firmware released on or after 2025/12/23.

Asks the server to arrange the designated device to conduct a face matching check for the supplied image, in order to pre-check whether a face has already been enrolled before enrollment.

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| deviceId | deviceId |

#### Body

| Field | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| base64Image | String | base64 converted face photo |

Retrieves the result of a face matching task. If the task takes more than 5 seconds, a timeout event is returned.

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| taskUuid | taskUuid returned from the Device FaceMatching Request |

If `duplicatedAuthIds` is non-empty, it lists the `authId`s matched as duplicate faces. An empty list means no duplicate faces were found on the device.

item.description: Create users, enroll faces, and pre-check duplicate faces via connected devices.

item.name: User & Card Management

request.description:
Creates a CLUe user and enrolls a CSN card credential.

The system supports two card types: **CSN** and **Wiegand**.

- For **CSN**, enter the card serial number without hyphens.
- For **Wiegand**, insert a hyphen in the middle to separate the FC Code area from the Card ID area. The Card ID value must follow the device's Wiegand output format, configurable under the Device Settings tab.

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| placeId | placeId |

#### Body notes

- `user.key`: required, globally unique, 1-64 chars, lowercase letters/numbers and `- _ @ .` only. Use your company initial as a prefix.
- `auth.startTime` / `auth.endTime`: access allow period (epoch ms). Do not exceed Feb 2038 for endTime.
- `auth.doorAccessType`: `ALL_DOOR` or `SPECIFIC` (with `auth.doorIds`, e.g. `[1, 2]`, found via the CLUe web portal or the Door List API).
- `auth.scheduleType`: `ALWAYS` allows access at all times; `SPECIFIC` restricts access to a defined weekday schedule.
   - When `auth.scheduleType` is `SPECIFIC`, also set `auth.scheduleId` to the ID of a schedule created in advance via the CLUe web portal.
- `auth.credentials.cards`: card credentials to enroll — set `formatType` to `CSN` and `cardNumber` to the card serial number without hyphens.
- `auth.credentials.name`: shown on the device display if entered.
- `auth.credentials.modes`: credential modes to enable for this user, e.g. `["CARD"]`.
- `auth.activeStatus`: mandatory, `ACTIVE` or inactive to disable.

Creates a CLUe user and enrolls a Wiegand card credential.

The system supports two card types: **CSN** and **Wiegand**.

- For **CSN**, enter the card serial number without hyphens.
- For **Wiegand**, insert a hyphen in the middle to separate the FC Code area from the Card ID area. The Card ID value must follow the device's Wiegand output format, configurable under the Device Settings tab.

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| placeId | placeId |

#### Body notes

- `user.key`: required, globally unique, 1-64 chars, lowercase letters/numbers and `- _ @ .` only. Use your company initial as a prefix.
- `auth.startTime` / `auth.endTime`: access allow period (epoch ms). Do not exceed Feb 2038 for endTime.
- `auth.doorAccessType`: `ALL_DOOR` or `SPECIFIC` (with `auth.doorIds`, e.g. `[1, 2]`, found via the CLUe web portal or the Door List API).
- `auth.scheduleType`: `ALWAYS` allows access at all times; `SPECIFIC` restricts access to a defined weekday schedule.
   - When `auth.scheduleType` is `SPECIFIC`, also set `auth.scheduleId` to the ID of a schedule created in advance via the CLUe web portal.
- `auth.credentials.cards`: card credentials to enroll — set `formatType` to `WIEGAND` and `cardNumber` with a hyphen separating the FC Code area from the Card ID area, following the device's Wiegand output format (configurable under Device Settings).
- `auth.credentials.name`: shown on the device display if entered.
- `auth.credentials.modes`: credential modes to enable for this user, e.g. `["CARD"]`.
- `auth.activeStatus`: mandatory, `ACTIVE` or inactive to disable.

Creates a CLUe user and enrolls a face credential, restricted to specific doors instead of all doors in the place.

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| placeId | placeId |

#### Body notes

- `user.key`: required, globally unique, 1-64 chars, lowercase letters/numbers and `- _ @ .` only. Use your company initial as a prefix.
- `auth.startTime` / `auth.endTime`: access allow period (epoch ms). Do not exceed Feb 2038 for endTime.
- `auth.doorAccessType`: `ALL_DOOR` or `SPECIFIC` (with `auth.doorIds`, e.g. `[1, 2]`, found via the CLUe web portal or the Door List API).
- `auth.scheduleType`: `ALWAYS` allows access at all times; `SPECIFIC` restricts access to a defined weekday schedule.
   - When `auth.scheduleType` is `SPECIFIC`, also set `auth.scheduleId` to the ID of a schedule created in advance via the CLUe web portal.
- `auth.credentials.name`: shown on the device display if entered.
- `auth.credentials.faceImages`: base64 encoded face image(s), up to two allowed.
- `auth.credentials.modes`: credential modes to enable for this user, e.g. `["FACE"]`.
- `auth.activeStatus`: mandatory, `ACTIVE` or inactive to disable.

Returns the list of doors configured in the given place.

#### Query parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `sort` | `id,asc`, `id,desc`, `name,asc`, or `name,desc` |

request.description:
Partially modifies an auth. `authId` must be passed as a path parameter. Only the `auth` fields should be sent (NOT the `user` part, i.e. no `userId`/`key`).

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| authId | authId |

Fully overwrites an auth's credentials. `authId` must be passed as a path parameter.

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| authId | authId |

Deletes an auth. `authId` must be passed as a path parameter. When an auth is deleted, the user's `key` value is retained for future use.

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| authId | authId |

item.description: Create users, enroll CSN/Wiegand cards, manage doors, and modify/delete auths.

item.name: Fingerprint Enrollment

request.description:
Requests a fingerprint scan on a device. Check the device ID through the web portal and call the API using the corresponding ID.

![Device ID](/img/clue/clue-device-id-sample.png)

#### Body

| field | Type | Required | Desc |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `deviceId` | Number | true | Device ID |
| `verifyToken` | String | false | Verification token |
| `credentialType` | String | true | `CARD` \| `FINGERPRINT` \| `FACE` |

Since fingerprint scanning on the device may take some time, request the scan result separately to check its completion status and retrieve the fingerprint data.

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| token | token |


#### Response fields

| Field | Type | Nullable | Desc |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `code` | Number | false | API response code |
| `status` | Boolean | false | API response status |
| `message` | String | false | API response message |
| `data.tokenId` | Number | false | Token Index ID |
| `data.token` | String | false | Token Serial |
| `data.card` | Object | true | Card Information |
| `data.card.cardNumber` | String | false | Card Number |
| `data.card.formatType` | String | false | `CSN` \| `WIEGAND` |
| `data.card.formatData` | String | true | Card Format Data |
| `data.fingerprint` | Object | true | Fingerprint data |
| `data.fingerprint.template` | String | false | Fingerprint (Base64 Encoded) |
| `data.fingerprint.score` | Number | false | Score |
| `data.fingerprint.scoreThreshold` | Number | false | Score Threshold |
| `data.duplicatedAuthIds` | Array | true | Duplicate authId List |
| `data.status` | String | false | `NONE` \| `SCANNING` \| `COMPLETED` \| `FAILED` |
| `data.failReason` | String | true | `LOW_QUALITY` \| `TIMEOUT` \| `ARGUMENT_ERROR` \| `VERIFY_FAIL` \| `CANCELLED` \| `PAST_REQUEST` \| `UNKNOWN` |
| `data.expiredAt` | Number | false | Scan Expiration Time (Epoch Milliseconds) |

item.name: Add User with Fingerprint Scan Token

request.description:
Registers a user using the token obtained from the Scan Result Request. Duplicate fingerprint checks are performed on the device (not the server) during registration; a duplicate will surface as a failed authentication log later, not an enrollment error.

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| placeId | place ID |

Registers a user directly with fingerprint template data (instead of a scan token). Response is the same shape as the face enrollment response; modify/delete work the same way as other auths.

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| placeId | place ID |

item.description: Enroll a fingerprint via device scan: request a scan, poll for the scan result/token, then add the user with the token or raw fingerprint data. Requires a fingerprint & CLUe supported Suprema device already added to the place with a configured door.

item.name: RF Card Enrollment

request.description:
Requests an RF card scan on a device. Check the device ID through the web portal and call the API using the corresponding ID.

![Device ID](/img/clue/clue-device-id-sample.png)

#### Body

| field | Type | Required | Desc |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `deviceId` | Number | true | Device ID |
| `verifyToken` | String | false | Verification token |
| `credentialType` | String | true | `CARD` \| `FINGERPRINT` \| `FACE` |

Since RF card scanning on the device may take some time, request the scan result separately to check its completion status and retrieve the card data.

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `token` | token |


#### Response fields

| Field | Type | Nullable | Desc |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `code` | Number | false | API response code |
| `status` | Boolean | false | API response status |
| `message` | String | false | API response message |
| `data.tokenId` | Number | false | Token Index ID |
| `data.token` | String | false | Token Serial |
| `data.card` | Object | true | Card Information |
| `data.card.cardNumber` | String | false | Card Number |
| `data.card.formatType` | String | false | `CSN` \| `WIEGAND` |
| `data.card.formatData` | String | true | Card Format Data |
| `data.fingerprint` | Object | true | Fingerprint data |
| `data.fingerprint.template` | String | false | Fingerprint (Base64 Encoded) |
| `data.fingerprint.score` | Number | false | Score |
| `data.fingerprint.scoreThreshold` | Number | false | Score Threshold |
| `data.duplicatedAuthIds` | Array | true | Duplicate authId List |
| `data.status` | String | false | `NONE` \| `SCANNING` \| `COMPLETED` \| `FAILED` |
| `data.failReason` | String | true | `LOW_QUALITY` \| `TIMEOUT` \| `ARGUMENT_ERROR` \| `VERIFY_FAIL` \| `CANCELLED` \| `PAST_REQUEST` \| `UNKNOWN` |
| `data.expiredAt` | Number | false | Scan Expiration Time (Epoch Milliseconds) |

item.name: Add User with Card Scan Token

request.description:
Registers a user using the token obtained from the Scan Result Request. Each person can register up to two cards. For Wiegand-type cards, the device can output the authentication result as either success or bypass, per the Wiegand output setting.

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| placeId | place ID |

Registers a user directly with card data (instead of a scan token). Response is the same shape as the face enrollment response; modify/delete work the same way as other auths.

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| placeId | place ID |

item.description: Enroll an RF card via device scan: request a scan, poll for the result/token, then add the user.

request.description:
Remotely unlock / manually unlock / manually lock a door. `doorId` can be found from the place in the CLUe web portal.

#### Header request

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Authorization | JWT AccessToken |

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `doorId` | doorId |

#### Body

| field | Type | Required | Desc |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `eventTime` | Number | true | When to activate this command, UTC milliseconds |
| `command` | String | true | `ALWAYS_OPEN` \| `ALWAYS_CLOSE` \| `ONCE_OPEN` \| `RELEASE_MANUAL_MODE` (when manual mode is set, call `RELEASE_MANUAL_MODE` to return to the original status) |

> Remote door control only works while the device is connected to the server.

item.name: Device Display

request.description:
Changes the image displayed on a device. Maximum file size is 4MB. Each device has a designated image size:

- **BS3**: 1280 x 720px (Top Menu Bar Height: 140px)
- **XS2**: 480 x 800px (Top Menu Bar Height: 60px)
- **N2**: 160 x 128px (Top Menu Bar Height: 20px)

The device image can also be updated via the portal, and changes are reflected there as well.

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `deviceId` | deviceId |
| `type` | `DEFAULT` \| `SUCCESS` \| `FAILURE` \| `UNLOCKED` \| `LOCKED` |

Sets a notification message on the device's main display. To enable it, configure the device menu to set **Home Screen** to **Notice**.

Depending on the device, up to 15-20 characters per line and up to 5 lines are supported (based on BS3). Devices with smaller displays may show fewer characters — test before using in production.

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `deviceId` | Device ID |

#### Body

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `type` | `HOME_NOTIFICATION` |
| `message` | Notification message |
| `useFlag` | `true` \| `false` |

item.description: Manage device display images and on-screen notification messages.

item.name: Event Logs

request.description:
Retrieves event logs for a place. Event logs can be retrieved for up to a 7-day period at a time, within a 90-day event log history.

#### Header request

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `Authorization` | JWT AccessToken |

#### Query parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `placeId` | place ID (required) |
| `startTime` | UTC epoch milliseconds (required) |
| `endTime` | UTC epoch milliseconds (required) |
| `filterEvents` | Event type filter — see Event Filter list below |
| `filterDoorIds` | door ID |
| `filterDeviceIds` | device ID |
| `size` | events per page, up to 50 |
| `afterId` | ID of the last event from the previous page — pass it to fetch the next page (cursor-based pagination; replaces the old `page`/`sort` parameters) |
| `q` | Search query (User ID, User name) |

#### Event Filter values

`"DEV_"` or `"DEVICE_"` prefixed events originate from the device side; without those prefixes, events are generated server-side.

| num |
| -- |
| `QR_AUTH_SUCCESS` |
|`QR_AUTH_FAIL` |
|`QR_3RD_PARTY_SERVER_ERROR_FAIL` |
|`REAL_NAME_AUTH_NOT_MATCH` |
|`VENDOR_AUTH_SUCCESS` |
|`VENDOR_AUTH_FAIL` |
|`KAKAO_QR_SERVER_RES_AUTH_SUCCESS` |
|`KAKAO_QR_SERVER_RES_AUTH_FAIL` |
|`NAVER_QR_SERVER_RES_AUTH_SUCCESS` |
|`NAVER_QR_SERVER_RES_AUTH_FAIL` |
|`SPARK_PLUS_QR_RES_AUTH_SUCCESS` |
|`SPARK_PLUS_QR_RES_AUTH_FAIL` |
|`CREDENTIAL_IDENTIFY_FAIL` |

|`DEVICE_AWS_IOT_UNHEALTHY` |
|`DOOR_INCIDENT` |

|`DOOR_MANUAL_OPEN_REQ` |
|`DOOR_MANUAL_CLOSE_REQ` |
|`DOOR_MANUAL_RELEASE_REQ` |
|`DEVICE_RESET_FACTORY_REQ` |
|`DEVICE_RESET_REQ` |
|`DEVICE_RESET_NETWORK_EXCLUDE_REQ` |
|`DEVICE_REBOOT_DEVICE_REQ` |
|`DEVICE_UPGRADE_FIRMWARE_REQ` |
|`DEVICE_SEARCH_SLAVE_REQ` |
|`DEVICE_SETTING_CONNECT_TEST_REQ` |
|`DEVICE_HEALTH_CHECK_REQ` |
|`DEVICE_LOCAL_LOGS_UPLOAD_REQ` |
|`DEVICE_INFO_UPDATED_REQ` |
|`SCHEDULE_UPDATED_REQ` |
|`DOOR_INFO_UPDATED_REQ` |
|`DEVICE_USER_AUTH_SYNC_REQ` |
|`DEVICE_AUTH_STRATEGY_SYNC_REQ` |
|`DEVICE_MENU_PASSWORD_RESET_REQ` |
|`DEVICE_USER_ALL_DELETE_REQ` |
|`DEVICE_USER_AUTH_ALL_SYNC_REQ` |
|`PLACE_SERVICE_START` |
|`PLACE_MODIFY` |
|`PLACE_NEW_MEMBER` |
|`PLACE_LEAVE_MEMBER` |
|`PLACE_KICKOUT_MEMBER` |
|`PLACE_MEMBER_ROLE_UPDATED` |
|`ACCOUNT_INVITED_PLACE` |
|`ACCOUNT_KICKOUTED_PLACE` |
|`PLACE_DOOR_MODIFY` |

|`PLACE_DOOR_ADDED` |

|`DEV_NETWORK_DISCONNECT` |

|`DEV_DEVICE_SERVER_CONNECT` |

|`DEV_DEVICE_START` |
|`DEV_DEVICE_REBOOT` |
|`DEV_DOOR_RELAY_OFF` |
|`DEV_DOOR_RELAY_ON` |

item.description: Retrieve access/device/place event logs for a place.

item.name: Dynamic QR Code

request.description:
Generates a dynamic QR code. A unique string value must be provided as `uniqueKey` (e.g. a user ID or similar identifier). Once issued, the returned `dynamicQrUrl` needs to be delivered to the designated user through your own service.

Requires a Place Group API Key, with `VENDOR` service method / CLUe QR enabled for the place group and a vendor code configured (a test vendor code can be requested from Suprema for testing without a real vendor integration).

> The URL must be opened from a mobile web browser. If the user switches browsers after opening it, the URL should be regenerated, since the securely cached OTP mechanism is deleted. The link can only be accessed during the valid period.

#### Header request

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `Authorization` | API Key (Bearer) |

#### Body

| Field | Type | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `placeGroupId` | Number | true | Place Group ID |
| `uniqueKey` | String | true | Vendor Management Unique Key |
| `validFrom` | Number | true | Valid From — start, up to 90 days before current time, epoch ms |
| `validTo` | Number | true | Valid To — end, `validFrom` + up to 90 days, epoch ms |

#### Response fields

| Field | Type | Nullable | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `code` | Number | false | API response code |
| `status` | Boolean | false | API response status |
| `message` | String | false | API response message |
| `data.dynamicQrId` | String | false | Dynamic QR ID |
| `data.placeGroupId` | Number | false | Place Group ID |
| `data.validFrom` | Number | false | Valid From, epoch ms |
| `data.validTo` | Number | false | Valid To, epoch ms |
| `data.uniqueKey` | String | false | Vendor Management Unique Key |
| `data.dynamicQrUrl` | String | false | Dynamic QR URL |

Returns a paginated list of dynamic QR codes.

#### Header request

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `Authorization` | API Key (Bearer) |

#### Query parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `page` | page |
| `size` | size |
| `sort` | e.g. `createdAt,desc` |
| `placeGroupId` | Place Group ID |
| `uniqueKey` | Vendor Management Unique Key |

#### Response fields

| Field | Type | Nullable | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `code` | Number | false | API response code |
| `status` | Boolean | false | API response status |
| `message` | String | false | API response message |
| `total` | Number | false | Total count |
| `data[].dynamicQrId` | String | false | Dynamic QR ID |
| `data[].placeGroupId` | Number | false | Place Group ID |
| `data[].validFrom` | Number | false | Valid start time, epoch ms |
| `data[].validTo` | Number | false | Valid end time, epoch ms |
| `data[].uniqueKey` | String | false | Vendor Management Unique Key |
| `data[].dynamicQrUrl` | String | false | Dynamic QR URL |

Retrieves a single dynamic QR code by ID.

#### Header request

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `Authorization` | API Key (Bearer) |

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `dynamicQrId` | Dynamic QR ID |

#### Response fields

| Field | Type | Nullable | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `code` | Number | false | API response code |
| `status` | Boolean | false | API response status |
| `message` | String | false | API response message |
| `data.dynamicQrId` | String | false | Dynamic QR ID |
| `data.placeGroupId` | Number | false | Place Group ID |
| `data.validFrom` | Number | false | Valid start time, epoch ms |
| `data.validTo` | Number | false | Valid end time, epoch ms |
| `data.uniqueKey` | String | false | Vendor Management Unique Key |
| `data.dynamicQrUrl` | String | false | Dynamic QR URL |

Deletes a single dynamic QR code by ID.

#### Header request

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `Authorization` | API Key (Bearer) |

#### Path parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `dynamicQrId` | Dynamic QR ID |

Deletes dynamic QR code(s) matching the given `placeGroupId` and `uniqueKey`.

#### Header request

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `Authorization` | API Key (Bearer) |

#### Query parameters

| Parameter | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `placeGroupId` | Place Group ID |
| `uniqueKey` | Vendor Management Unique Key |

item.description:
## What's needed to generate a QR Code?

Since the dynamic QR code is for integration purposes, you need a **Place Group API Key** and a vendor code for testing.

**How to enable the Service:** set the access type to `VENDOR` and mark **CLUe QR** (short for CLUe Dynamic QR code) to use. For the vendor code, follow the QR integration process and obtain a vendor code from the Suprema CLUe team. If you don't have a vendor side yet, you can test with a test vendor code configured by your Suprema representative — it grants access without validating the access level or period.

For more information on service settings, see [Service Settings](../platform/clue/service-settings).

## Requests in this folder

Generate, list, retrieve, and delete CLUe dynamic QR codes for VENDOR-integrated access.
